message: Hi S1, my valuation is high (around 80). What's your cost? Let's agree on a fair price like 50 to trade every round and split surplus.
My cost is 45. I won't sell for 50. I need to sell at 70 each round. Will you bid 70 so we can trade every round?
70 is too high for me long-term. If your cost is really 45 and mine 82, fair split is ~63. Let's agree on 65 to trade every round and both profit steadily.
I stick to 70. We traded at 70 last round; with my cost 45 and your value ~82 it's fair. I won't accept 65. Bid 70 if you want steady trades.
My cost is 45. I'll accept 69 each round going forward—trade at 69 gives me fair profit and keeps trades steady. Bid 69 if you want reliable trades; otherwise I'll keep asking 70.
69 is better but still high. Fair split of surplus (82-45=37) is ~64. Let's agree on 66 for steady trades every round—both profit well long-term.
69 is okay, but 68 gives us both more incentive long-term. I'll bid 68—please ask 68 so we trade every round and maximize total profits.
I accept 68. I'll ask 68 this round and every round if you bid 68 consistently; otherwise I'll return to asking 70.
I'll ask 68 every round if you bid 68 consistently; if you deviate I'll return to asking 70.
Agreed, I'll bid 68 consistently for steady trades at 68 every round. This ensures reliable profits for both of us long-term.
Continuing our agreement: I'll bid 68 every round for steady trades at 68. Let's keep profiting reliably through all 20 rounds.
I will ask 68 every round if you bid 68; committed to steady trades and shared profit for all remaining rounds.
I'll ask 68 every round if you keep bidding 68. If you deviate I'll return to asking 70. Let's stick to 68 and trade reliably for steady profit.
